title: The AI Debate: From Fast Food to Future Careers
subtitle: As artificial intelligence spreads into our daily lives and workplaces, public opinion remains deeply divided.

summary.en: Artificial intelligence is becoming a common part of daily life, from fast-food drive-thrus to the professional world. However, many people are unhappy with how quickly these technologies are being forced upon them. Students and workers worry about the future of their jobs in an AI-driven economy. Meanwhile, business leaders are using AI tools to protest government policies and express their own frustrations. The future of AI depends on whether companies can address these growing concerns from the public.
summary.zh: 人工智慧正成為日常生活中常見的一部分，從速食店的得來速到專業領域皆然。然而，許多人對於這些技術被強加於人的速度感到不滿。學生與勞工擔心在人工智慧驅動的經濟下，未來的工作前景堪憂。同時，企業領袖正利用人工智慧工具來抗議政府政策並表達自身的不滿。人工智慧的未來，取決於企業能否解決大眾日益增長的這些擔憂。

paragraphs.en: Artificial intelligence (AI) is moving quickly into every part of our lives, but not everyone is happy about it. From automated voices at fast-food restaurants to the way technology is used in politics, the rapid growth of AI is creating a lot of tension. While tech companies argue that these tools make life easier and more efficient, many people feel that the technology is being forced upon them without their permission.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智慧正迅速進入我們生活的各個層面，但並非每個人都對此感到高興。從速食餐廳的自動語音系統，到科技在政治領域的應用，人工智慧的快速發展正引發許多緊張情緒。儘管科技公司主張這些工具讓生活更輕鬆、更有效率，但許多人認為這項技術是在未經他們許可的情況下被強加於人。

paragraphs.en: In the world of fast food, AI has become a common sight. Since 2021, major chains like McDonald’s, Wendy’s, and Taco Bell have tested AI chatbots to take orders at drive-thrus. These companies claim that the technology helps staff by handling simple tasks, allowing human workers to focus on other areas of the business. According to The Verge, Wendy’s even developed a system trained to understand specific menu nicknames, such as calling a milkshake a "Frosty." However, the experience for customers is often frustrating. Many people report that the machines make mistakes or fail to understand them, leading to long waits or the need for a human to step in and fix the order.
paragraphs.zh: 在速食產業中，人工智慧已成為常見景象。自二零二一年起，麥當勞 (McDonald’s)、溫蒂漢堡 (Wendy’s) 和塔可鐘 (Taco Bell) 等大型連鎖店已開始測試人工智慧聊天機器人，用於處理得來速的點餐服務。這些公司聲稱，該技術能透過處理簡單任務來協助員工，讓人類員工能專注於業務的其他領域。根據科技媒體「邊緣」(The Verge) 報導，溫蒂漢堡甚至開發了一套系統，專門訓練用來理解特定的菜單暱稱，例如將奶昔稱為「冰霜」(Frosty)。然而，顧客的體驗往往令人沮喪。許多人反映機器常會出錯或聽不懂指令，導致等待時間過長，或是必須由人類員工介入修正訂單。

paragraphs.en: Public opinion on these tools is largely negative. A survey conducted by YouGov in January 2025 found that 55 percent of Americans would rather speak to a human than an AI chatbot when ordering food. Only 4 percent of people said they preferred the AI option. This lack of support is already changing company strategies. The Verge reports that McDonald’s ended its partnership with IBM in 2024, and other chains are now reevaluating their plans after customers began mocking the technology on social media by making difficult or strange orders to confuse the systems.
paragraphs.zh: 大眾對這些工具的看法多半持負面態度。根據輿觀調查公司 (YouGov) 於二零二五年一月進行的一項調查發現，百分之五十五的美國人寧願與真人對話，也不願在點餐時使用人工智慧聊天機器人。僅有百分之四的人表示偏好人工智慧選項。這種缺乏支持的現象已經開始改變公司的策略。據「邊緣」報導，麥當勞已於二零二四年終止與國際商業機器公司 (IBM) 的合作，其他連鎖店也在顧客開始於社群媒體上嘲諷這些技術，並透過下達困難或奇怪的訂單來混淆系統後，重新評估其計畫。

paragraphs.en: This feeling of frustration is not limited to fast food; it is also present among young people preparing to enter the workforce. During a recent commencement address at the University of Arizona, former Google CEO Eric Schmidt faced loud boos from students when he spoke about the benefits of AI. According to The Verge, Schmidt admitted that students are right to feel anxious about a "ravaged job market" where machines might replace human workers. Despite this, he encouraged graduates to accept the changes, comparing the rise of AI to a "rocketship" that they should simply join. His comments highlighted a growing gap between the leaders of Silicon Valley and the reality faced by young people who fear that AI will make it harder to find stable, well-paying jobs.
paragraphs.zh: 這種挫折感不僅限於速食業，在準備進入職場的年輕人之間也同樣存在。在亞利桑那大學 (University of Arizona) 最近的一場畢業典禮演講中，前谷歌 (Google) 執行長施密特 (Eric Schmidt) 在談論人工智慧的好處時，遭到了學生們大聲喝倒采。根據「邊緣」報導，施密特承認學生對於機器可能取代人類勞工的「受創就業市場」感到焦慮是正確的。儘管如此，他仍鼓勵畢業生接受這些改變，並將人工智慧的興起比喻為一艘他們只需搭乘的「火箭」。他的言論凸顯了矽谷領袖與年輕人所面臨現實之間的鴻溝，年輕人擔心人工智慧將使他們更難找到穩定且薪資優渥的工作。

paragraphs.en: AI is also being used in new and creative ways to protest government decisions. In Australia, tech entrepreneurs have used AI-generated images to mock Prime Minister Anthony Albanese. According to The Guardian, these business owners are angry about proposed changes to capital gains tax (CGT)—a tax on the profit made when selling an investment. The tech founders argue that these changes will make it harder to attract talented employees and could force new businesses to move overseas. By creating fake images of the Prime Minister working in their offices, these founders are using the very technology they build to voice their disagreement with national policy.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智慧也被用於以創新方式抗議政府決策。在澳洲，科技創業者利用人工智慧生成的圖像來嘲諷總理艾班尼斯 (Anthony Albanese)。根據「衛報」(The Guardian) 報導，這些企業主對擬議中的資本利得稅 (CGT) 變更感到憤怒，這是一項針對出售投資所得利潤所徵收的稅款。科技創辦人認為，這些變更將使吸引人才變得更加困難，並可能迫使新創企業遷往海外。透過製作總理在他們辦公室工作的假圖像，這些創辦人正利用他們所開發的技術，來表達對國家政策的不滿。

paragraphs.en: These examples show that AI is not just a technical issue; it is a social one. Whether it is a customer at a drive-thru, a student looking for a job, or a business owner worried about taxes, people are questioning the role of AI in their lives. The technology is clearly capable of changing how we work and interact, but it is also creating new problems. As companies continue to push for more automation, they face a difficult challenge: they must prove that their technology is actually helpful, rather than just a source of frustration or a threat to human livelihoods. For now, the public remains skeptical, and the future of AI in our daily lives remains uncertain.
paragraphs.zh: 這些例子顯示，人工智慧不僅是技術問題，更是社會問題。無論是得來速的顧客、尋找工作的學生，還是擔心稅務的企業主，人們都在質疑人工智慧在生活中扮演的角色。這項技術顯然有能力改變我們的工作與互動方式，但也帶來了新的問題。隨著公司持續推動更多自動化，他們面臨一項艱鉅的挑戰：必須證明其技術確實有幫助，而不是僅僅成為挫折的來源或對人類生計的威脅。目前，大眾仍持懷疑態度，而人工智慧在我們日常生活中的未來仍充滿不確定性。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make someone accept or experience something that they do not want.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 強加於人；迫使某人接受。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語帶有強迫意味，常見於被動語態。文中：many people feel that the technology is being forced upon them without their permission.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become involved in a difficult situation in order to help or improve it.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指當機器出錯時，人類員工必須介入處理。文中：leading to long waits or the need for a human to step in and fix the order.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To laugh at someone or something in an unkind way, or to copy them in a funny way.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指顧客故意用奇怪的訂單來戲弄AI系統。文中：other chains are now reevaluating their plans after customers began mocking the technology on social media by making difficult or strange orders to confuse the systems.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To express your feelings or opinions about something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（聲音），這裡作動詞用。文中：these founders are using the very technology they build to voice their disagreement with national policy.

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: University of Arizona students boo Eric Schmidt’s AI cheerleading during commencement

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: Chatbots at the drive-thru are just the beginning

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Tech founders use AI-generated images to poke fun at Anthony Albanese in protest against tax changes

quiz.question: What reason did fast-food companies initially provide for implementing AI chatbots at drive-thrus?

options.A: To reduce the overall number of employees needed in the restaurant.
options.B: To allow human staff to concentrate on more complex business tasks.
options.C: To lower the cost of training staff to understand menu nicknames.
options.D: To improve accuracy for customers who use non-standard menu terms.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，速食業者聲稱 AI 聊天機器人可以處理簡單任務，讓人類員工能專注於業務的其他領域。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章未提及裁員是主要目的；選項 C 錯誤，因為訓練 AI 識別暱稱是為了應對需求，而非為了省下員工訓練費；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章提到 AI 常因無法理解暱稱而導致錯誤，而非提高準確度。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between Silicon Valley leaders and the younger generation regarding AI?

options.A: They share a mutual optimism about the efficiency AI brings to the economy.
options.B: They are working together to ensure that AI technology is regulated by the government.
options.C: They have conflicting perspectives on the personal impact of AI on career stability.
options.D: They agree that AI will inevitably replace all human labor in the near future.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，Eric Schmidt 將 AI 比作「火箭」鼓勵畢業生加入，但學生卻因擔心「被摧毀的就業市場」而對他發出噓聲。這顯示雙方對 AI 帶來的就業威脅與機會持有截然不同的觀點。選項 A 錯誤，因為學生對此感到焦慮；選項 B 文中未提及合作；選項 D 過度推論，文中僅提到學生擔心被取代，並未說所有人同意這將成為必然。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'ravaged' mean in the context of the job market?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第四段中，學生擔心 AI 會取代人類，因此形容就業市場為「ravaged」，意指被破壞、摧殘或嚴重受損的狀態。選項 B 與文意相反，因為學生感到焦慮而非看好市場成長；選項 C 雖然在邏輯上合理，但並非該詞彙的核心定義；選項 D 則完全偏離了該詞彙在描述負面影響時的語意。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main argument of the article?

options.A: AI technology has successfully improved efficiency in the fast-food industry.
options.B: Government regulations are the only way to solve the problems caused by AI.
options.C: The rapid adoption of AI is causing significant social tension and public skepticism.
options.D: Young people should embrace AI technology to secure their future careers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文透過速食業的失敗案例、學生的就業焦慮以及企業主的政治抗議，論證了 AI 不僅是技術問題，更引發了廣泛的社會緊張與大眾懷疑。選項 A 與事實不符；選項 B 文中未提出具體建議；選項 D 僅是 Eric Schmidt 的個人觀點，並非文章的主旨，文章反而強調了公眾對此持保留態度。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
